title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. xanthochlora
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. xanthochlora, also known as the lady's mantle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This herb is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful for wound healing and soothing irritated skin. The health benefits of Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. xanthochlora include reducing menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-aging effects. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a cream or ointment.
